hero.name: Florencia
hero.title: Research Fellow · Information Systems Engineering
hero.description: Empirical software engineering researcher with experience in agile estimation, systematic literature reviews, and international innovation projects. Passionate about software quality and AI-assisted engineering, with a strong problem-solving mindset and continuous learning orientation.

about.label: About
about.title: Learn more about my journey
about.subtitle: Advanced Information Systems Engineering student with a research profile.
about.bio1: Final-year Information Systems Engineering student at UTN (Argentina) with research experience in empirical software engineering, agile estimation, and systematic literature reviews. I have contributed to multiple research projects focused on software quality and agile team productivity, co-authored scientific publications, and participated in international innovation competitions. My work involves collaboration in academic and international research environments, with a strong focus on continuous learning and adaptation to new technologies.
about.bio2: My work spans academic research, survey design and analysis, interdisciplinary collaboration, and AI-assisted approaches for agile estimation. I am particularly interested in AI-assisted software development, agile methodologies, and empirical software engineering.

items.desc: Personal CV website built with Next.js, TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. Modern design with dark mode, bilingual support and automated deployment.

details.longDesc: Professional portfolio developed as a personal project to showcase my background, skills, and projects. Implements Next.js 16 App Router architecture with i18n, light/dark themes, Framer Motion animations, and responsive design. Data is managed through next-intl translation files, allowing easy content scaling.

details.results: A fully functional, deployable portfolio that presents my professional profile interactively and accessibly from any device.

items.title: HarmonIA — Huawei ICT Competition
items.desc: AI and IoT solution for mental health support. Global finalist in the Innovation track of the Huawei ICT Competition.

details.longDesc: HarmonIA is an integrated solution combining artificial intelligence and the Internet of Things to provide mental health support. The project was developed by an interdisciplinary team and presented at the Huawei ICT Competition, reaching the global finals.
details.results: 2nd Place Global Finalist, 1st Place LATAM and 1st Place Argentina in Innovation Track of Huawei ICT Competition 2025.

items.title: SLR: Agile Software Estimation
items.desc: Systematic literature review on agile software estimation, with publications at JAIIO and CONAIISI.

details.longDesc: Research focused on effort estimation in agile software development environments. Multiple systematic literature reviews were conducted to identify practices, challenges, and improvement opportunities in agile estimation, culminating in publications at national and international conferences.

details.results: 4 scientific publications at JAIIO and CONAIISI (2023-2025). Generation of a preliminary framework for improving agile estimation.

items.title: Quality-in-Use Analysis of E-commerce Web Platforms
items.desc: Final project for the Software Product and Process Quality course (UTN, 2024). We developed a comprehensive quality evaluation model based on QuAM and ISO 25000, applied to Mercado Libre with real users to measure understandability, effectiveness, and satisfaction in use.
items.pdfUrl: /Aspectos avanzados de la calidad de software TPI.pdf

details.longDesc:
This integrative practical project originated in the Software Product and Process Quality course (UTN, 2024) with a clear goal: to build a concrete tool for measuring the quality in use of ecommerce platforms from the end-user perspective.

We began by studying the state of the art in software quality models, adopting the ISO/IEC 25000 framework and the QuAM (Quality Agile Model) as our foundation. From there, we selected three quality characteristics that we considered critical for the success of an online shopping platform:

• **Understandability** — How intuitive is the navigation? Can a new user complete a purchase without assistance?
• **Effectiveness in use** — Does the user achieve their goals accurately and without errors?
• **Satisfaction in use** — Does the experience generate trust and a positive perception?

Each characteristic was broken down into subcharacteristics and measurable attributes. For example, for Satisfaction in use we defined the "Trust" subcharacteristic, with attributes such as "Perception of personal data security" and "Clarity of return policies". For each attribute we designed quantitative metrics with evaluation scales, acceptance criteria, and relative weights.

The next step was to apply the model to a real case: Mercado Libre, the largest ecommerce platform in Latin America. We recruited real users, designed a structured survey linked to each attribute of the model, and analyzed the results using descriptive statistical methods.

The findings were revealing: we identified significant strengths in overall navigation ease, but also concrete areas for improvement in shipping information transparency, clarity of exchange policies, and payment security perception. Each finding was documented with its corresponding metric support, and we proposed actionable recommendations.

The final result was a 49-page technical report integrating the theoretical framework, model design, evaluation methodology, case study results, and recommendations. Beyond the deliverable, the process gave us deep insights into how to translate abstract quality standards into concrete metrics and how to design empirical studies that generate real value for product teams.
features: Built a QuAM quality model adapted for ecommerce platforms from ISO 25000
Selected and justified 3 characteristics: Understandability, Effectiveness in use, and Satisfaction in use
Defined subcharacteristics (ease of reading, ease of help, trust) with attributes and quantitative metrics
Designed a structured survey directly linked to the model's attributes
Applied the model to Mercado Libre with real users
Descriptive statistical analysis of results and generation of recommendations
49-page technical report integrating theory, model, methodology, and results
details.results: We built a functional and replicable quality model for evaluating ecommerce platforms, successfully applied it to Mercado Libre with real users, and generated a 49-page report with concrete findings and actionable recommendations. The project was approved as the final assessment for the course and demonstrated that international quality standards can be translated into practical evaluation tools.
